pitch.title: Open House
pitch.logline: A ruthless real estate agent discovers her latest luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity that feeds on potential buyers, forcing her to market the deadly property to the worst people she knows to satisfy its hunger.
pitch.core_theme: The moral bankruptcy of corporate greed and the modern housing crisis.
pitch.synopsis:
Act I: Valerie is a cutthroat realtor on the verge of bankruptcy. She lands the exclusive listing for a stunning, modernist mansion. During the first showing, the house literally consumes a wealthy, obnoxious client. Instead of calling the police, Valerie realizes the house produces pristine, blood-free diamonds after every meal.

Act II: Valerie strikes a dark pact with the house. She begins hosting exclusive, invite-only open houses for corrupt politicians, rival cutthroat realtors, and toxic tech bros. The house gets fed, and Valerie gets rich. However, the house's appetite grows exponentially, and it begins demanding more innocent souls, trapping Valerie inside during a massive gala.

Act III: Realizing she has become the ultimate monster, Valerie decides to destroy the property. She locks herself in with her most ruthless corporate boss and triggers a gas leak. In a chaotic, bloody finale, the house tries to digest them both, but Valerie ignites the gas, blowing the mansion to kingdom come and ending the cycle of greed.

beats.description: Valerie, a sharply dressed, utterly ruthless real estate agent, aggressively evicts a struggling family to secure a minor commission, establishing her absolute moral bankruptcy.

Valerie discovers her brokerage is about to fire her due to mounting secret debts. Therefore, she uses blackmail to steal the agency's crown jewel: an exclusive listing for a bizarre, stunning modernist mansion.
Valerie sets up a private showing at the mansion for an obnoxiously wealthy, abusive client. The client insults the property and threatens to ruin Valerie's career.

The mansion's floorboards suddenly liquefy, swallowing the client whole in a gruesome, silent gulp. Valerie panics and prepares to call the police, but the house regurgitates a pristine, blood-free diamond. Therefore, Valerie makes a dark choice to keep quiet and profit.
Valerie forms a symbiotic pact with the house. She curates a hit list of the city's worst elites, including corrupt politicians and toxic tech bros, inviting them to private showings.
The house devours the terrible clients one by one in creatively horrific ways. Therefore, Valerie collects a fortune in diamonds, solving her debt and ascending to the top of her brokerage.
Valerie enjoys her new lavish lifestyle. But the house begins to rumble and crack, unsatisfied with the meager portions of single clients. It demands more sustenance.

The house holds Valerie hostage during a routine cleaning, refusing to unlock the doors until she promises a massive feast. Therefore, Valerie is forced to organize an exclusive, invite-only gala at the property to feed it a buffet of corporate scumbags.

act_2.sequence_number_and_title: Sequence 5: The Gala of the Damned

beats.description: The gala begins. Valerie packs the house with rival realtors, her awful boss, and wealthy criminals. At first, the house discreetly picks off guests in isolated rooms.

The house loses its patience and drops the facade. It seals all the exits and begins a mass consumption, turning the modernist architecture into a shifting, deadly labyrinth of teeth and crushing walls.
Valerie tries to navigate the chaos, assuming the house will spare her. But the entity turns its attention to the innocent catering staff and Valerie's overworked, underpaid assistant.

The house corners Valerie and demands she push her innocent assistant into a gaping maw to save herself. Valerie realizes her own greed has made her the ultimate monster. Therefore, she refuses the house's demand, breaking the pact.
Valerie decides to destroy the property. She uses her intimate knowledge of the house's floor plan to guide her assistant and the innocent staff to a structural weak point to escape.

Her ruthless corporate boss realizes what is happening and tries to sacrifice Valerie to the house. Therefore, Valerie locks herself in the basement with her boss, engaging in a brutal fistfight while the house tries to digest them both.

Valerie overpowers her boss and uses a heavy wrench to smash the mansion's main gas line. As the house screams and the walls close in to crush her, she sparks a lighter and dives into a reinforced panic room.

The gas ignites, blowing the monstrous mansion to kingdom come. Valerie emerges from the smoldering crater, battered but alive. She finds a single diamond in the rubble, tosses it into the ashes, and walks away, finally ending the cycle of greed.

characters.name: Valerie Vance
characters.brief_summary: A razor-sharp, morally bankrupt real estate agent drowning in secret debt. She serves as the anti-hero whose journey from ruthless capitalist to self-sacrificing survivor anchors the film's critique of greed.

psychological_core.wound: Grew up in extreme poverty, watching her parents lose their home to predatory lenders.
psychological_core.false_belief: Wealth is the only armor against victimization, and morality is a luxury for the rich.
psychological_core.fear: Returning to poverty, powerlessness, and being discarded by society.
psychological_core.desire: To be untouchably wealthy and sit at the absolute top of the food chain.
psychological_core.need: To realize human life has inherent value beyond financial utility.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Fast, transactional, and heavily reliant on corporate buzzwords like 'pivot' and 'value add.' She NEVER says 'I'm sorry' or 'I can't afford it.'
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Immediately pivots to discussing property values, market comps, or aggressively pointing out the other person's professional shortcomings.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Appears highly polished, charming, and composed 90% of the time. Only when cornered or financially threatened does her veneer crack, revealing a feral, panicked desperation.

characters.name: Richard Sterling
characters.brief_summary: The sociopathic, impeccably tailored head of Valerie's brokerage who views human beings strictly as capital. He is the human embodiment of the house's insatiable greed and the final physical obstacle Valerie must overcome.

psychological_core.wound: Inherited his wealth but was constantly told by his self-made father that he lacked the 'killer instinct.'
psychological_core.false_belief: Empathy is a market inefficiency that must be eradicated to achieve true greatness.
psychological_core.fear: Being outsmarted, rendered obsolete by his subordinates, or exposed as a fraud.
psychological_core.desire: Complete monopolization of the luxury real estate market at any human cost.
psychological_core.need: To be consumed by the very predatory system he champions.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in calm, measured, patronizing metaphors about apex predators and market forces. He NEVER raises his voice or admits to being surprised.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Answers direct questions with condescending philosophical inquiries about capitalism and 'the natural order.'

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Maintains a genial, fatherly facade in the office. His sociopathy only peeks through in dead-eyed stares and micro-aggressions when he feels his authority is questioned.

characters.name: Toby Aris
characters.brief_summary: Valerie's chronically overworked, deeply empathetic assistant who dreams of building affordable housing. He acts as Valerie's B-Story Anchor and moral compass, constantly challenging her transactional view of humanity.

psychological_core.wound: Was evicted as a teenager and knows the trauma of housing insecurity firsthand.
psychological_core.false_belief: If I work hard enough for bad people, I can change the system from the inside.
psychological_core.fear: Being complicit in the harm of innocent people.
psychological_core.desire: To earn enough capital and experience to start a non-profit housing trust.
psychological_core.need: To stop enabling toxic abusers and stand up for his own worth.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Self-deprecating, speaks in run-on sentences, and constantly apologizes. He NEVER says 'No' directly to Valerie until the climax.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Physically organizes things like shuffling papers or cleaning lint while changing the subject to minor logistical details.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Highly competent and professional in front of clients. His anxiety and moral discomfort only leak out in nervous tics, like adjusting his glasses or clearing his throat, when Valerie crosses an ethical line.

characters.name: Julian Vance
characters.brief_summary: A sleazy, flash-in-the-pan rival realtor who relies on nepotism and social media followers rather than skill. He serves as the 'Skeptic' who investigates Valerie's sudden success, threatening to expose her dark secret.

psychological_core.wound: Deeply insecure about his lack of actual talent, knowing he is a fraud riding his family's coattails.
psychological_core.false_belief: Perception is reality; if I look successful, I am successful.
psychological_core.fear: Being exposed as a talentless hack and losing his social status.
psychological_core.desire: To steal Valerie's listing and humiliate her publicly.
psychological_core.need: To face the brutal reality of his own inadequacy.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Drops names constantly, uses internet slang like 'vibes' and 'crushing it,' and speaks with unearned bravado. He NEVER admits he doesn't know something.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Laughs loudly and mocks the other person's outfit or social standing to deflect from his own ignorance.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Projects absolute confidence 90% of the time. When his competence is genuinely challenged, his smile tightens and he aggressively checks his phone to avoid eye contact.

characters.name: Councilwoman Elena Rostova
characters.brief_summary: A corrupt, untouchable city politician who zones low-income neighborhoods for luxury development. She acts as the 'Harbinger' and catalyst for the House's escalating hunger, representing the systemic rot Valerie is feeding.

psychological_core.wound: Spent her early career as an idealistic public defender but was crushed by the corrupt system, deciding to join them rather than beat them.
psychological_core.false_belief: Power is the only currency that matters in this city.
psychological_core.fear: Losing her grip on the city's political machine and becoming irrelevant.
psychological_core.desire: To acquire the mansion as a symbol of her absolute dominion over the city.
psychological_core.need: To face the karmic consequences of the lives she has destroyed.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in legalistic, evasive political double-speak with very deliberate pacing. She NEVER makes a definitive promise or uses the word 'bribe.'
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Feigns moral outrage or invokes 'the good of the community' when accused of self-interest.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Impeccably polite and charming in public. Her true ruthlessness only shows in the cold, unblinking way she stares down opponents when the cameras are off.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Establishes the Sin (greed) that will eventually invite the wrath of the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Cold detachment and moral bankruptcy.
beats.pacing_notes: Sharp, aggressive, and highly transactional to establish the protagonist's ruthless baseline.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie Vance, impeccably dressed and devoid of empathy, oversees the eviction of a struggling family from a modest home to secure a meager commission. She ignores the tears of the former owners, aggressively pivoting the conversation to market comps and property values when confronted. This establishes her absolute moral bankruptcy and her deeply ingrained false belief that wealth is the only shield against victimization.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Highlights the moral failing and false belief that the Monster will exploit.
beats.emotional_arc: Annoyance masking a deep-seated fear of vulnerability.
beats.pacing_notes: A brief, dialogue-heavy pause that grounds the thematic argument of the film.
beats.detailed_action: Back at the luxury brokerage, Valerie's overworked and highly empathetic assistant, Toby Aris, timidly suggests that human beings have inherent value beyond their financial utility. Valerie immediately shuts him down with a barrage of corporate buzzwords, asserting that morality is a luxury only the ultra-rich can afford. This exchange plants the central thematic question of whether human life is worth more than capital, highlighting the systemic rot of corporate greed.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist commits the Sin out of desperation, setting her on a collision course with the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Feral panic hidden beneath a polished, professional exterior.
beats.pacing_notes: Mounting tension as the protagonist's safe world threatens to collapse.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie is called into the glass-walled office of her sociopathic boss, Richard Sterling, who calmly informs her that her secret, mounting debts have become a liability and she is on the verge of termination. Desperate to maintain her armor of wealth, Valerie discovers a scandalous secret about a rival agent and ruthlessly blackmails them to steal the agency's crown jewel. She secures the exclusive listing for a bizarre, stunning modernist mansion, setting the stage for her descent into literal and figurative darkness.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster reveals itself within the House, disrupting the protagonist's reality.
beats.emotional_arc: Absolute shock and primal terror.
beats.pacing_notes: Sudden, violent escalation that shatters the grounded corporate reality.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie hosts a private showing at the modernist mansion for an obnoxiously wealthy, emotionally abusive client who relentlessly insults the property and threatens to ruin Valerie's career. Without warning, the sleek hardwood floorboards liquefy into a fleshy maw, swallowing the screaming client whole in a gruesome, silent gulp. Valerie is thrown into a state of absolute shock, realizing her luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist rationalizes the Sin, debating whether to flee the House or feed the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Moral hesitation overridden by desperate greed.
beats.pacing_notes: Suspenseful, ticking-clock hesitation as a life-altering choice is weighed.
beats.detailed_action: Trembling with panic, Valerie pulls out her phone and prepares to dial the police to report the monstrous event. However, before she can make the call, the house shudders and regurgitates a pristine, blood-free diamond onto the floor. Valerie stares at the gemstone, mentally weighing her crushing financial ruin against the horrific murder she just witnessed, wrestling with the ultimate moral compromise.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist fully commits to the Sin, actively choosing to co-exist with the Monster in the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Cold resolve and the crossing of a moral point of no return.
beats.pacing_notes: Deliberate, heavy, and final beat that locks the narrative into the second act.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie slowly lowers her phone, picks up the diamond, and wipes it clean, making a dark, unspoken pact with the entity. She chooses to keep quiet, actively stepping into the upside-down world of being a monster's caretaker to solve her financial woes. The sin of greed is fully embraced, locking her into the literal and figurative Monster in the House dynamic.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the antithesis of the Sin (empathy) through a character who represents salvation.
beats.emotional_arc: Guilt masked by transactional generosity and false confidence.
beats.pacing_notes: Warm but fundamentally uneasy, providing a breather before the carnage.
beats.detailed_action: Flush with cash from her first diamond, Valerie pays off her debts and interacts with Toby, who is thrilled by her sudden success and hopes she will finally fund his dream of a non-profit affordable housing trust. Valerie uses Toby's deep knowledge of the city's corrupt elites to unwittingly curate a hit list of terrible clients, masking her predatory intentions as ambitious networking. Toby acts as her moral anchor, constantly reminding the audience of the humanity Valerie is sacrificing to feed her own ambition.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Sin seems to pay off as the Monster consumes the wicked, fulfilling the promise of the premise.
beats.emotional_arc: Giddy, sociopathic triumph and intoxicating power.
beats.pacing_notes: Fast, rhythmic, darkly comedic montage of escalating horror and wealth.
beats.detailed_action: A darkly comedic montage unfolds as Valerie hosts a series of exclusive, invite-only open houses for the city's worst elites, including corrupt politicians like Councilwoman Rostova and toxic tech bros. The house devours these terrible clients one by one in creatively horrific ways, turning modernist architecture into deadly, shifting traps. Valerie collects a fortune in regurgitated diamonds, ascending to the very top of her brokerage while gleefully ignoring the mounting moral toll of her actions.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster grows too powerful to control; the Sin officially traps the protagonist inside the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Sudden shift from arrogant control to terrifying captivity.
beats.pacing_notes: An abrupt halt to the fun and games, spiking anxiety and raising the stakes.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie revels in her lavish new lifestyle, believing she has completely mastered the beast and achieved a false victory of ultimate wealth. However, during a routine cleaning, the house begins to rumble, crack, and bleed, unsatisfied with the meager portions of single clients. It violently seals the doors, holding Valerie hostage until she promises to organize a massive, invite-only gala to feed it a buffet of corporate scumbags, proving she is the pet, not the master.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster turns on everyone; the House drops its disguise and becomes a literal death trap.
beats.emotional_arc: Claustrophobic dread and the total loss of control.
beats.pacing_notes: Accelerating chaos as multiple threats converge on the protagonist simultaneously.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie packs the mansion with rival realtors, wealthy criminals, and her awful boss, Richard Sterling, desperately trying to orchestrate a controlled feeding to appease the entity. The sleazy rival realtor, Julian Vance, becomes suspicious of Valerie's success and begins snooping around the property, threatening to expose her dark secret to the world. The house loses its patience with the slow drip of victims, dropping its architectural facade to reveal shifting, fleshy walls and sealing all the exits to begin a mass consumption.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster threatens the innocent; the true, devastating cost of the Sin is revealed.
beats.emotional_arc: Crushing guilt and the death of the ego.
beats.pacing_notes: Frantic, breathless survival mode that culminates in a devastating realization.
beats.detailed_action: As the modernist mansion transforms into a deadly labyrinth of teeth and crushing walls, Valerie tries to navigate the chaos, arrogantly assuming the house will spare its provider. To her absolute horror, the entity ignores the corrupt elites and turns its insatiable hunger toward the innocent catering staff and her fiercely loyal assistant, Toby. Valerie experiences the whiff of death as she realizes her unmitigated greed has unleashed an unstoppable force that is about to slaughter the only decent person in her life.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist must choose between continuing the Sin or saving her soul by fighting the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Agonizing self-reflection and absolute despair.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, heavy, and intensely intimate amidst the surrounding carnage.
beats.detailed_action: The house corners Valerie and Toby on a collapsing staircase, opening a gaping, acidic maw directly beneath them. The entity telepathically demands that Valerie push her innocent assistant into the pit to save herself and continue their lucrative partnership. Staring into Toby's terrified eyes, Valerie is forced to confront her deepest psychological wound, finally realizing that her pursuit of wealth has turned her into the exact same predatory monster as her boss, Richard.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist repents of the Sin and decides to destroy the Monster and the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Righteous defiance and the spark of redemption.
beats.pacing_notes: Explosive burst of proactive energy as the protagonist finally takes a moral stand.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie violently rejects the house's demand, grabbing Toby by the collar and pulling him back from the edge of the maw. She loudly declares that she is breaking the lease, actively choosing human life over infinite wealth and severing her symbiotic pact with the monster. Armed with her intimate knowledge of the mansion's floor plan, she takes charge to lead Toby and the surviving innocent staff toward a structural weak point in the basement.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The ultimate battle against the Monster and the physical embodiment of the Sin (Richard) within the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Cathartic release of rage against the corrupt system.
beats.pacing_notes: High-octane, brutal, and explosive climax.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie successfully guides the innocent staff to an escape vent, but Richard Sterling intervenes, realizing the house's nature and attempting to sacrifice Valerie to secure the diamond-producing property for himself. A brutal, bloody fistfight erupts between Valerie and her sociopathic boss in the mansion's mechanical room while the house's walls literally close in to crush them both. Valerie overpowers Richard, uses a heavy wrench to smash the main gas line, sparks a lighter, and dives into a reinforced panic room just as the entity lunges for her.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Sin is permanently rejected; the Monster and House are destroyed, leaving a changed protagonist.
beats.emotional_arc: Peaceful liberation and spiritual rebirth.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, quiet, and resolute, contrasting sharply with the opening image.
beats.detailed_action: The ignited gas causes a massive explosion, blowing the monstrous mansion and Richard Sterling to kingdom come in a spectacular shower of debris. Valerie emerges from the smoldering crater, battered, bloody, but entirely alive and free from her debts. She spots a single, massive diamond resting in the ashes, picks it up for a fleeting moment, and then intentionally tosses it back into the rubble before walking away with Toby, finally ending the cycle of greed.

beats.emotional_arc: Peaceful liberation and spiritual rebirth.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, quiet, and resolute, contrasting sharply with the opening image.
beats.detailed_action: The ignited gas causes a massive explosion, blowing the monstrous mansion and Richard Sterling to kingdom come in a spectacular shower of debris. Valerie emerges from the smoldering crater, battered, bloody, but entirely alive and free from her debts. She spots a single, massive diamond resting in the ashes, picks it up for a fleeting moment, and then intentionally tosses it back into the rubble before walking away with Toby, finally ending the cycle of greed.

stage5_treatment.title_logline_characters:
TITLE: Open House
GENRE: Horror Comedy
LOGLINE: A ruthless real estate agent discovers her latest luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity that feeds on potential buyers, forcing her to market the deadly property to the worst people she knows to satisfy its hunger.
CHARACTERS:
VALERIE VANCE: A razor-sharp, morally bankrupt real estate agent drowning in secret debt.
RICHARD STERLING: The sociopathic, impeccably tailored head of Valerie's brokerage who views human beings strictly as capital.
TOBY ARIS: Valerie's chronically overworked, deeply empathetic assistant who dreams of building affordable housing.
JULIAN VANCE: A sleazy, flash-in-the-pan rival realtor who relies on nepotism and social media followers rather than skill.
COUNCILWOMAN ELENA ROSTOVA: A corrupt, untouchable city politician who zones low-income neighborhoods for luxury development.
stage5_treatment.act_1:
The morning sun offers no warmth to the manicured lawn of a modest suburban home, where VALERIE VANCE (34) stands like a perfectly tailored sentinel of doom. Impeccably dressed in a sharp designer suit, her face is a mask of cold detachment as she oversees the forced eviction of a struggling family. Movers ruthlessly haul cardboard boxes and worn furniture onto the curb, dismantling a life while Valerie checks her luxury watch, annoyed by the inefficiency of human despair.

The former homeowner, a tearful mother clutching a toddler, approaches Valerie in a desperate plea for just a few more days to gather their belongings. Valerie does not flinch, nor does she offer a single syllable of apology. Instead, her eyes remain dead and focused on the property's facade, calculating the exact cost of the cosmetic renovations required to flip the house.

Without a shred of empathy, Valerie aggressively pivots the conversation away from the woman's tears, launching into a rapid-fire lecture about market comps and property values. She coldly explains that this eviction is a necessary value add for the neighborhood's overarching financial health. In her mind, the interaction is purely transactional, cementing her deeply ingrained false belief that wealth is the absolute only shield against becoming a victim in a merciless world.

The sterile, glass-and-steel environment of the luxury brokerage serves as a stark contrast to the messy human emotion of the morning. Here, Valerie reigns supreme, striding past the desks of lesser agents while her chronically overworked assistant, TOBY ARIS (26), scurries to keep pace. Toby, deeply empathetic and visibly anxious, nervously adjusts his glasses and shuffles a stack of listing agreements, trying to physically organize his moral discomfort.

Unable to hold his tongue any longer, Toby timidly brings up the evicted family, stammering out a gentle suggestion that human beings possess an inherent value that extends far beyond their financial utility. He hopes to appeal to a shred of humanity buried beneath Valerie's polished exterior, desperately wanting to believe that the system they work for can be changed from the inside.

Valerie immediately shuts him down with a suffocating barrage of corporate buzzwords, instructing him to pivot his mindset and focus on their deliverables. She leans in close, her voice dropping to a sharp, venomous whisper as she delivers her ultimate thematic punchline: morality is a luxury that only the ultra-rich can afford. The exchange hangs in the cold air of the office, drawing a thick line in the sand between Toby's fragile idealism and Valerie's absolute moral bankruptcy.

The illusion of Valerie's untouchable success shatters when she is abruptly summoned into the cavernous, glass-walled office of her boss, RICHARD STERLING (55). Richard, a sociopathic apex predator in a bespoke suit, calmly pours himself a scotch while staring dead-eyed at the city skyline. He never raises his voice, speaking in measured, patronizing metaphors about market forces and the natural order of the food chain.

With chilling precision, Richard reveals that he is fully aware of Valerie's secret, mounting debts, noting that her financial instability has become a liability to his firm. He delivers a calm but devastating ultimatum: she is on the verge of termination, about to be cast out into the very poverty she has spent her entire life running from. Beneath her carefully composed veneer, Valerie experiences a surge of feral, panicked desperation, her greatest fear of powerlessness suddenly staring her in the face.

Cornered and fighting for her corporate survival, Valerie immediately goes on the offensive, targeting a sleazy, nepotism-fueled rival agent named JULIAN VANCE (32). Knowing Julian relies entirely on internet slang and unearned bravado to mask his incompetence, Valerie uncovers a scandalous secret about his fraudulent escrow accounts. She ruthlessly blackmails him in the agency breakroom, forcing him to hand over the firm's crown jewel: an exclusive listing for a bizarre, isolated modernist mansion, setting the stage for her descent into a literal nightmare.

The modernist mansion sits isolated against a backdrop of dense, shadowy woods, its sleek lines and cold glass exuding an aura of unnatural perfection. Valerie hosts a private, high-stakes showing for an obnoxiously wealthy tech bro, a client whose emotional abuse is as vast as his bank account. He struts through the pristine hallways, relentlessly insulting the property's architecture and threatening to ruin Valerie's career over the lack of smart-home integration.

Valerie maintains her plastic, professional smile, absorbing the client's toxic vitriol while mentally calculating the commission that will save her from ruin. The client stomps his designer shoes against the sleek hardwood floorboards, loudly declaring the house to be a hollow, worthless piece of garbage. The tension in the cavernous living room reaches a boiling point, the silence of the isolated property amplifying his cruel, echoing laughter.

Without a single warning, the physical reality of the room violently fractures. The sleek hardwood floorboards beneath the client's feet suddenly liquefy, peeling back to reveal a massive, fleshy maw lined with jagged, impossible teeth. In a gruesome, silent gulp, the house swallows the screaming billionaire whole, snapping its wooden jaws shut and instantly returning to a pristine, polished state. Valerie is thrown against the glass wall, paralyzed by absolute shock and primal terror as she realizes her luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity.

The deafening silence of the empty mansion rings in Valerie's ears, broken only by her own ragged, panicked breathing. Trembling violently, she scrambles backward away from the center of the room, her manicured hands fumbling to pull her cell phone from her designer blazer. Her thumb hovers agonizingly over the emergency dial pad, every instinct screaming at her to call the police and flee this architectural nightmare.

Before she can press the screen, the entire house emits a deep, guttural shudder that vibrates through the soles of her shoes. The floorboards in the center of the room ripple like water, and with a sickening, wet sound, the house regurgitates a single object onto the pristine wood. It lands with a heavy, crystalline clink, rolling to a stop just a few feet away from where Valerie cowers.

Valerie creeps forward, her breath catching in her throat as she stares down at a massive, flawless diamond, completely free of blood or biological residue. The ticking clock of her crushing financial ruin violently collides with the horrific reality of the murder she just witnessed. She stares at the gemstone, her mind racing as she weighs the absolute certainty of bankruptcy and homelessness against the dark, impossible wealth sitting right in front of her.

The harsh glow of the phone screen illuminates Valerie's conflicted face, the numbers 9-1-1 glaring back at her like a final moral test. The police are just one button press away, a choice that would end her career, expose her debts, and strip away her armor of wealth forever. But the diamond sparkles in the dim light of the mansion, whispering a promise of salvation, power, and the ultimate escape from victimization.

Slowly, deliberately, the feral panic drains from Valerie's eyes, replaced by a cold, calculating resolve. She lowers the phone, letting the screen fade to black, and drops to her knees on the very spot where the client was consumed. She reaches out, her fingers wrapping around the cold, heavy gemstone, and casually uses the sleeve of her expensive blazer to wipe a faint trace of ethereal saliva from its brilliant facets.

Valerie stands tall in the center of the silent, hungry room, the diamond clutched tightly in her fist like a weapon. She sweeps her gaze across the sleek, modernist walls, an unspoken, dark pact forging between the ruthless capitalist and the malevolent entity. She has actively chosen her path, fully embracing the sin of greed and stepping across the moral point of no return to become the caretaker of a monster.
stage5_treatment.act_2a:
Flush with untraceable cash from the liquidation of her first regurgitated diamond, Valerie pays off her crushing secret debts in a single, euphoric wire transfer. The suffocating weight of her financial ruin vanishes overnight, replaced by a dangerous, bulletproof arrogance. She struts into the sterile brokerage like a newly minted queen, pausing at Toby's cluttered desk to toss down a ridiculously expensive, transactional gift—a designer watch meant to buy his continued loyalty and mask her own lingering guilt.

Toby is absolutely thrilled by her sudden windfall, adjusting his glasses as his eyes shine with genuine, misplaced hope. Believing Valerie has finally secured the leverage needed to change the firm, he eagerly pitches his ultimate dream: a non-profit affordable housing trust funded by their luxury commissions. Valerie nods with a practiced, plastic smile, pretending to care about his noble crusade while a much darker realization dawns on her. Toby possesses an encyclopedic, heavily researched knowledge of the city's most corrupt, untouchable elites—the exact dietary requirements of her new architectural pet.

Under the guise of ambitious networking, Valerie manipulates Toby into curating a highly specific hit list of terrible clients. Toby eagerly complies, pulling up dossiers on slumlords and embezzlers, believing Valerie is finally using her influence to disrupt the corrupt system from the inside. He constantly reminds her of the human cost of their industry, acting as an unwitting moral anchor while Valerie secretly sizes up his targets for slaughter, perfectly blending her predatory intentions with corporate ambition.

A macabre, high-fashion slaughterhouse disguised as a luxury real estate campaign begins in earnest. Valerie hosts a series of exclusive, invite-only open houses, specifically targeting the monsters on Toby's meticulously researched list. COUNCILWOMAN ELENA ROSTOVA (50), dripping in stolen municipal funds and designer jewelry, sneers at the mansion's marble countertops before a pristine kitchen island suddenly splits open, dragging the corrupt politician screaming into a churning, fleshy garbage disposal of jagged drywall and rebar.

The carnage escalates into a darkly comedic rhythm of architectural horrors, transforming the modernist masterpiece into a shifting, deadly trap. A toxic crypto-billionaire is suffocated by a sentient, shrinking infinity pool; a predatory hedge fund manager is crushed flat by a rapidly descending vaulted ceiling that resets itself without a single drop of blood spilled. After each gruesome consumption, the house dutifully regurgitates a flawless, fist-sized diamond onto the immaculate floorboards, which Valerie collects with giddy, sociopathic triumph.

Valerie ascends to the absolute pinnacle of Richard Sterling's brokerage, her desk overflowing with congratulatory floral arrangements and vintage champagne. She completely ignores the mounting moral toll of her actions, intoxicated by the sheer power of weaponizing a malevolent entity for her own financial gain. The sudden disappearances of the city's worst elites are easily chalked up to spontaneous offshore vacations, leaving Valerie untouchable, wealthy beyond measure, and completely blind to the monster's rapidly expanding appetite.

Believing she has completely mastered the beast, Valerie revels in her lavish new lifestyle, sipping champagne while overseeing a routine, solitary cleaning of the modernist mansion. She treats the sentient entity like a tamed guard dog, a perfect, deadly machine that prints money on command. But as she casually wipes a speck of dust from a floating glass banister, a deep, unnatural groan vibrates through the foundation, instantly shattering her illusion of absolute control.

The pristine white walls suddenly crack like dry skin, seeping thick, black blood that ruins the imported Persian rugs. The house writhes and shudders, clearly unsatisfied with the meager, sporadic portions of single clients. With a violent, deafening slam, every heavy oak door and reinforced glass window in the mansion seals shut simultaneously, plunging the cavernous living room into terrifying, claustrophobic darkness.

Trapped and hyperventilating, Valerie pounds her manicured fists against the unbreakable glass, her arrogant control evaporating into primal, feral panic. The house violently shifts its floorboards, physically squeezing her toward the gaping, fleshy maw in the center of the room until she screams out a desperate, breathless bargain. She promises to organize a massive, invite-only gala, a veritable buffet of corporate scumbags to satisfy its ravenous hunger, realizing with chilling certainty that she is not the master of the house, but its captive pet.
stage5_treatment.act_2b:
The night of the gala arrives, transforming the sentient mansion into a glittering, high-stakes buffet. Valerie orchestrates the feeding with ruthless precision, packing the sprawling estate with the absolute worst of the city's elite, including RICHARD STERLING (55), her predatory, untouchable boss. Valerie plays the perfect hostess, her smile tight and her eyes darting nervously as she tries to guide the worst white-collar offenders toward the hungriest rooms. She glides through the crowd in a stunning crimson gown, a visual echo of the blood she is prepared to spill, desperately trying to manage the flow of the victims to appease the rumbling, impatient foundation beneath her designer stilettos.

The carefully curated feeding frenzy hits a massive snag in the form of JULIAN VANCE (38), a sleazy, cocaine-fueled rival realtor who has been tracking Valerie’s meteoric rise with venomous envy. Julian slips away from the champagne-swilling crowd, snooping through the restricted master suite and tearing through Valerie's hidden files. He corners her in a dimly lit hallway, brandishing a folder of missing persons reports and property anomalies, his breath reeking of expensive gin. He threatens to blow her entire empire wide open, demanding a massive cut of whatever illicit operation she is running out of the property.

But Julian’s blackmail is cut brutally short. The house, entirely devoid of patience for Valerie’s slow, methodical drip of victims, decides to take matters into its own hands. The sleek, architectural facade violently drops. The pristine white drywall suddenly ripples like bruised flesh, and the ambient recessed lighting flickers into a sickly, pulsating crimson. With a synchronized, ear-splitting slam, every single exit—from the grand mahogany double doors to the floor-to-ceiling patio glass—seals shut with thick, bone-like deadbolts. The mass consumption begins, trapping the city's most corrupt elite inside a literal, breathing death trap.

The modernist masterpiece instantly devolves into a chaotic, fleshy labyrinth of grinding teeth and crushing, shifting walls. Screams echo through the cavernous living room as a group of embezzling tech billionaires are swallowed whole by a suddenly liquefying sectional sofa. Valerie navigates the blood-soaked carnage with a terrifying, bulletproof arrogance, assuming her status as the house's provider grants her absolute immunity. She strides through the shifting corridors, dodging snapping floorboards, fully expecting the entity to gorge itself on the corrupt elites and leave her to collect the regurgitated diamonds in peace.

Her sociopathic confidence shatters into absolute horror when the house abruptly changes its dietary preferences. Bypassing a cowering Richard Sterling, the entity’s fleshy tendrils whip across the room, snatching up the terrified, underpaid catering staff. The innocent servers are dragged screaming into the ceiling vents, their silver trays of hors d'oeuvres clattering against the blood-stained marble. The monster is no longer a surgical instrument of vigilante justice; it is a gluttonous, indiscriminate beast, and its hunger has mutated far beyond the sinful.

The true, devastating cost of Valerie's sin crystallizes when the house sets its sights on TOBY (26). Her fiercely loyal assistant, who spent the evening naively pitching his affordable housing trust to the trapped billionaires, is suddenly cornered by a rapidly narrowing hallway of jagged, calcified drywall. Valerie watches the walls close in on the only decent person in her life, the crushing weight of her unmitigated greed finally suffocating her ego. She experiences the undeniable, putrid whiff of death, realizing her ambition has unleashed an apocalyptic force that is about to slaughter the very innocence she used as a moral shield.

Frantic and stripped of all her glamorous armor, Valerie dives through the shifting, fleshy corridors, grabbing Toby by the collar just as the walls attempt to crush him flat. She drags him toward the grand floating staircase, but the house anticipates her move. The glass steps violently buckle and collapse, trapping them on a narrow, trembling landing. Beneath them, the polished hardwood splits wide open, revealing a gaping, acidic maw lined with rows of translucent, jagged teeth. The stench of sulfur and digested greed billows up from the pit, choking them both as the house corners its provider.

A deep, guttural vibration rattles through Valerie’s skull, a telepathic demand from the entity that bypasses her ears and strikes directly at her soul. The house offers her a sickeningly simple ultimatum: push the innocent, terrified assistant into the churning acid below, and she can walk out the front door with her life, her wealth, and their lucrative partnership intact. The entity pulses with a dark, seductive energy, promising her the absolute pinnacle of the real estate world if she just sheds this final ounce of dead weight.

Valerie stares into Toby’s wide, tear-filled eyes, his hands trembling as he clings to her crimson gown for salvation. In his terrified reflection, she sees the horrifying truth of her own identity. She is forced into an agonizing, intimate self-reflection amidst the deafening screams of the dying gala. The psychological wound of her past financial ruin, the desperation that drove her to this madness, suddenly feels utterly hollow. She realizes with absolute, despairing clarity that her relentless pursuit of wealth has not saved her; it has simply transformed her into the exact same predatory, soul-sucking monster as Richard Sterling.
stage5_treatment.act_3:
The guttural vibration of the entity's ultimatum still rattles through VALERIE (34) as she stares down into the acidic, tooth-lined chasm. The house pulses with a sickly, seductive warmth, promising her an empire of regurgitated diamonds if she just lets go of TOBY (26). Instead, a fierce, righteous defiance ignites within her chest. She violently yanks Toby backward by the lapels of his cheap suit, hauling him away from the snapping, translucent teeth. The house shrieks in response, a deafening wail of twisting metal and tearing drywall that shakes the very foundation. Valerie stands tall against the shifting architecture, shouting into the cavernous maw that she is breaking the lease, permanently severing her symbiotic pact with the gluttonous beast.

The mansion immediately retaliates, transforming from a passive trap into an active predator. The grand staircase fully collapses into a slide of jagged glass and splintered wood, but Valerie’s intimate, obsessive knowledge of the floor plan becomes her ultimate weapon. She grabs Toby’s hand and sprints through the collapsing foyer, dodging snapping floorboards and raining chandeliers. She remembers the original blueprints, specifically the structural vulnerabilities she once exploited to hide the mansion's grisly secret. She leads Toby toward the servant's corridors, a narrow labyrinth the entity has largely ignored in its feast.

Along the way, they stumble upon a cluster of terrified, blood-spattered catering staff huddled behind a shattered marble island in the kitchen. The walls around them are beginning to secrete a corrosive, digestive acid, slowly melting the stainless steel appliances. Valerie takes absolute command, her previous sociopathic arrogance replaced by a desperate, protective fury. She herds the surviving innocents through the melting pantry, kicking down a reinforced door that leads down into the subterranean mechanical room. The entity roars, sending fleshy tendrils whipping down the stairwell, but Valerie manages to slam the heavy steel door shut, buying them a temporary reprieve in the damp, vibrating basement.

In the dim, flickering light of the mechanical room, Valerie quickly directs the weeping staff toward an old, rusted ventilation shaft that leads directly to the exterior hillside. Toby helps hoist the servers into the narrow escape route, the sounds of the house tearing itself apart echoing from above. Just as Valerie turns to follow them, a heavy iron pipe swings out from the shadows, smashing into her ribs. She collapses to the concrete floor, gasping for air, as RICHARD STERLING (55) steps into the dim light. Her predatory boss is covered in the blood of his colleagues, his expensive tuxedo torn, but his eyes are wide with a manic, terrifying greed. He has deduced the nature of the house and the source of Valerie's sudden wealth, and he has zero intention of letting the golden goose die.

Richard declares that the property is too valuable to abandon, gleefully attempting to drag Valerie back toward the basement door to offer her up as a peace offering to the hungry architecture. A brutal, bloody fistfight erupts in the cramped, pulsating mechanical room. Valerie fights with the feral desperation of a woman who has already lost her soul and is clawing it back inch by inch. She dodges Richard’s wild swings, driving her knee into his stomach and slamming his head against the vibrating water heater. The house, sensing the conflict, begins to shrink the room, the concrete walls literally closing in to crush them both into a fine paste.

The space grows claustrophobically tight, the grinding of the shifting foundation threatening to deafen them. Richard tackles Valerie, his hands wrapping tightly around her throat as he tries to choke the life out of her, screaming that the real estate market belongs to the ruthless. Valerie’s vision blurs, but her flailing hand finds a heavy, cast-iron wrench abandoned on the floor. With a final surge of adrenaline, she brings the wrench crashing down onto Richard’s skull, shattering his grip. She scrambles away from his bleeding, dazed form, her eyes locking onto the mansion's massive, industrial gas main.

Knowing the entity is closing in for the final kill, Valerie swings the wrench with all her might, smashing the main gas line valve. A deafening hiss fills the shrinking room as thick, volatile fumes pour into the enclosed space. Richard stumbles to his feet, coughing and screaming in protest as the fleshy, tooth-lined walls finally burst through the concrete, lunging toward them. Valerie pulls a silver lighter from her crimson gown, sparks the flint, and tosses it directly into the geyser of gas. Without looking back, she dives headfirst into the mansion's reinforced, titanium-lined panic room, slamming the heavy vault door shut just as the entity's maw snaps shut on Richard Sterling.

The ignited gas triggers a catastrophic, earth-shattering explosion that rips through the subterranean levels and tears the modernist masterpiece apart from the inside out. The blast wave obliterates the fleshy labyrinth, incinerating the grinding teeth, the shifting walls, and the manic Richard Sterling in a spectacular, cleansing firestorm. The detonation blows the roof clean off the foundation, sending a shower of burning debris, shattered glass, and pulverized drywall raining down across the pristine Hollywood Hills. Inside the panic room, Valerie is thrown violently against the titanium walls, the world outside erupting into a blinding, roaring inferno that finally silences the demonic heartbeat of the house.

Hours later, the smoke begins to clear, revealing a smoldering, blackened crater where the architectural marvel once stood. The heavy vault door of the panic room groans open, and Valerie emerges into the cool morning air. She is battered, covered in soot, and her stunning crimson gown is reduced to blood-stained rags, but she is entirely alive. The oppressive, suffocating weight of her debts and her ambition has vanished, replaced by a profound, peaceful liberation. She climbs out of the wreckage, her lungs filling with the crisp, clean air of a new dawn, completely free from the monstrous cycle she had perpetuated.

As she navigates the charred rubble, a glint of light catches her eye. Resting perfectly intact amidst the gray ashes is a single, massive diamond, a final, tempting regurgitation from the destroyed beast. Valerie pauses, reaching down to pick up the flawless stone. She rolls it between her fingers for a fleeting moment, feeling its cold, heavy promise of infinite wealth. Then, with a quiet, resolute exhale, she intentionally tosses the diamond back into the smoldering ruins. She turns her back on the fortune, walking away from the crater to join Toby, who waits for her on the distant hillside, ready to rebuild her life from the ground up.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODEST SUBURBAN HOME - FRONT LAWN - MORNING
scenes.narrative_action: Movers ruthlessly haul cardboard boxes and worn furniture onto the curb. Valerie Vance stands perfectly still, checking her luxury watch. A tearful mother clutching a toddler approaches, begging for more time. Valerie doesn't make eye contact; she physically steps around the woman, pointing at the home's facade and dictating cosmetic renovation costs and market comps into her phone.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Opening Image. Establishes Valerie's extreme moral bankruptcy, her transactional view of humanity, and the central sin of greed.
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MODEST SUBURBAN HOME - FRONT LAWN - MORNING

A faded plastic tricycle hits the damp grass. Hard. 

Heavy work boots stomp past it. 

TWO BURLY MOVERS haul a stained mattress out the front door of a peeling-paint ranch house. They toss it onto a growing mountain of cardboard boxes and black trash bags bleeding into the street.

Standing in the eye of this storm is VALERIE VANCE (30s). 

She wears a razor-sharp, stark white power suit. Not a single hair out of place. She taps the face of a heavy gold Rolex. 

A beat. She adjusts the watch strap. It is a fraction too tight, biting into her wrist. 

BRENDA (30s), eyes red, hair a bird's nest of panic, clutches a crying TODDLER to her chest. She chases the movers down the concrete steps.

BRENDA
Wait, please! The fragile box, my mother's china is in there--

A mover carelessly drops a taped box. The distinct SOUND of shattering porcelain echoes across the lawn. 

Brenda gasps, dropping to her knees. She looks up, desperately searching for an authority figure. 

Her eyes lock onto Valerie. 

Brenda scrambles to her feet, shifting the toddler to her hip, and rushes toward the woman in white.

BRENDA
Are you from the bank? Please. My husband gets his bonus on Friday. We can catch up on the arrears, I swear. I just need until Friday.

Valerie doesn't blink. She doesn't even look at Brenda’s face. Her gaze is fixed on the rotting wood of the porch. 

Valerie lifts a sleek smartphone. Taps the screen. 

VALERIE
(into phone)
Toby. Property on Elm. The facade is a tear-down. We need to pivot on the curb appeal immediately. 

Brenda steps closer.

BRENDA
Did you hear me? We have nowhere to go. The shelters are full. 

Valerie smoothly steps to the left, physically flowing around Brenda like a matador avoiding a clumsy bull. She points at the roofline.

VALERIE
(into phone)
Rip out the dead azaleas, pour fresh concrete for the driveway. Paint the brick charcoal. Factor in twenty-five k for superficial rehab. 

BRENDA
You can't just throw us on the street! We've lived here for six years! My son was born in that room!

Brenda reaches out. Her fingers graze the pristine white sleeve of Valerie's blazer.

Valerie stops dead. 

She slowly lowers the phone. She stares at the spot where Brenda touched her. A micro-flinch of absolute disgust ripples across her jaw. 

She brushes the fabric. Once. Sharply. 

VALERIE
(into phone)
The bones are decent, but the current aesthetic is actively depressing the market value. We need a modern value-add before we list. 

Brenda begins to openly sob, the toddler wailing in harmony.

BRENDA
Look at me! Please, just look at my baby! Have a heart!

Valerie finally turns her head. She looks at Brenda. A perfectly placid, empty smile stretches across her face. 

VALERIE
Toby, pull the comps for the new builds on Maple. If we stage this aggressively, we can list at six-fifty. 

Valerie steps past the weeping mother, moving closer to the debris pile. 

VALERIE
The neighborhood is gentrifying. We just have to scrub the distress off the lot. 

Valerie hits 'send' on the voice note. She slides the phone into her pocket and locks eyes with the HEAD MOVER.

VALERIE
Keep the staging area clear. The photographer is here at noon. I want this debris out of frame by eleven.

HEAD MOVER
Yes, ma'am. 

The mover tosses another box onto the pile. A framed family photo spills out, glass cracking against the asphalt. 

Valerie checks her Rolex one last time. 

She turns her back on the mother. Her white heels click rhythmically against the pavement. 

She walks toward a sleek black SUV parked at the curb. She never looks back.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - BULLPEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie power-walks through the sterile, glass-and-steel office. Her assistant, Toby, scurries to keep up, nervously shuffling a stack of listing agreements. Toby stops walking, stammering out a plea about the evicted family's inherent human value. Valerie spins around, crowds his personal space, and delivers a venomous whisper: 'Morality is a luxury.' She snatches the files from his hands and marches off.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Theme Stated. Plants the central thematic question regarding the value of human life versus capital, while establishing Toby as the moral compass.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - RICHARD'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Richard Sterling stands with his back to Valerie, calmly pouring a scotch while staring out the floor-to-ceiling windows. He lists off Valerie's secret debts with chilling precision. Valerie's posture stiffens; her hands grip the back of a leather chair so hard her knuckles turn white. Richard turns, takes a sip, and delivers his ultimatum: she is on the verge of termination. Valerie's composed veneer cracks into a wide-eyed, feral panic.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Setup/Inciting Incident of the Sequence. Strips away Valerie's armor of wealth, triggering her deep-seated fear of poverty and forcing her into desperate action.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - BREAKROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Julian Vance is aggressively laughing into his phone while making an espresso. Valerie enters, slams the door shut, and corners him against the counter. She pulls out a tablet displaying his fraudulent escrow accounts and shoves it into his chest. As Julian's smug smile vanishes and he nervously checks his phone to avoid eye contact, Valerie physically rips a sleek black folder—the exclusive mansion listing—out of his hands.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Protagonist takes aggressive action. Shows the lengths Valerie will go to survive, securing the cursed property that will become her doom.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DRIVEWAY - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stands alone in front of the isolated, cold-glass mansion, flanked by dense, shadowy woods. A deafeningly loud sports car tears up the driveway. An obnoxious Tech Bro steps out, immediately screaming into his Bluetooth earpiece. He slams the car door, stomps up to Valerie, and starts berating her about the property's lack of cell service before even saying hello.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the Monster's lair and the first 'victim.' Establishes the Tech Bro as highly unsympathetic, prepping the audience for the horror-comedy tone.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - HALLWAYS - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: The Tech Bro struts aggressively through the pristine, echoing hallways, swatting at the sleek light fixtures and loudly insulting the architecture. Valerie trails a few steps behind, her face frozen in a painful, plastic smile. She grips her clipboard tightly, absorbing his toxic vitriol while desperately trying to pivot the conversation to smart-home integration.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Builds tension. Escalates the victim's awful behavior and highlights Valerie's humiliating desperation to close the deal at any cost.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: The Tech Bro stands in the center of the cavernous room, stomping his designer shoes on the hardwood and declaring the house a 'worthless piece of garbage.' Suddenly, the floorboards beneath him liquefy. A massive, fleshy maw lined with jagged teeth erupts from the wood, swallowing the screaming billionaire whole in a single, wet gulp. The floor instantly snaps back to pristine wood. The shockwave throws Valerie violently against the glass wall.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Monster is revealed. A massive genre pivot from corporate drama to supernatural horror, answering the dramatic question of what the house truly is.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Dead silence. Valerie scrambles backward on her hands and knees, trembling violently. She fumbles her phone out of her blazer pocket and punches in 9-1-1, her thumb hovering over the call button. The entire room emits a deep, guttural shudder. The center floorboards ripple, and the house regurgitates a heavy, flawless diamond. It rolls across the wood, clinking to a stop inches from Valerie's knees.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Complication. Presents Valerie with a literal manifestation of her ultimate desire born from horrific violence, forcing a moral crisis.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stares at the glittering diamond, then down at the glowing 9-1-1 on her phone screen. Her panicked breathing slows. She deliberately presses 'Cancel' and lets the phone drop to the floor. She crawls forward, picks up the heavy gemstone, and casually uses the sleeve of her designer blazer to wipe away a strand of ethereal saliva. She stands up tall, clutching the diamond, and surveys the hungry room with a cold, calculating smirk.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Point of No Return. Valerie actively chooses greed over morality, sealing her dark pact with the entity and launching Act II.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's luxury sedan crunches onto the pristine gravel driveway of the isolated, glass-and-steel mansion surrounded by dense, shadowy woods. She steps out, power-walking to the massive front door. She punches a code into the smart-lock. The heavy door unseals with a pneumatic hiss. Valerie steps inside, aggressively adjusting blinds and flicking on light switches. The house emits a low, guttural hum that vibrates the floor-to-ceiling windows. Valerie dismisses it as a noisy HVAC system, pulling a bottle of expensive room freshener from her designer bag and spraying it to mask a faint, metallic scent hanging in the air.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes the Monster's lair. Highlights Valerie's willful ignorance of literal red flags as she prioritizes the aesthetics of the sale over her own safety.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DRIVEWAY - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: A neon-green sports car screeches to a halt, kicking gravel onto Valerie's shoes. BRENT (30s, obnoxious Tech Bro) kicks his door open, screaming at his mother over a Bluetooth headset. He stomps toward the entrance, deliberately snapping a solar landscaping light with his sneaker. Valerie forces a painful, plastic smile, extending a complimentary bottle of artisan water. Brent ignores her hand, knocking the bottle to the asphalt where it shatters. He barges past her into the house without breaking his phone conversation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the first victim. Establishes Brent as aggressively toxic and unsympathetic, prepping the audience for the horror-comedy tone so his death feels earned.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Brent aggressively taps his metal car keys against the imported marble countertops, leaving visible scratches. He loudly complains about the lack of smart-appliances, demanding to know why the house is so 'dumb.' Valerie mentally calculates her commission, swallowing her pride and smoothly pivoting into a rehearsed pitch about the 'minimalist, unplugged aesthetic.' Behind Brent's back, a heavy oak cabinet door slowly, silently opens like a hungry mouth. The moment Brent turns around, the cabinet snaps shut with a violent bang. Brent jumps, blaming Valerie for a draft.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalates tension. The house begins to physically react to Brent's toxic presence, building supernatural dread while Valerie remains focused purely on the transaction.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - MASTER BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Brent twists the handles of the rainfall shower, scoffing at the water pressure. He leans over the massive, sunken soaking tub. The silver drain gurgles loudly, emitting a wet, choked sound like a throat clearing. Valerie nervously checks her tablet, rapidly talking over the noise to blame the plumbing on an 'eco-friendly water reclamation system.' Brent steps uncomfortably close to Valerie, invading her personal space, and whispers that she is going to have to 'try a lot harder' if she wants his money.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Raises the personal stakes. Brent shifts from annoying to physically intimidating, emphasizing Valerie's desperate, humiliating position and pushing the scene toward its boiling point.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GREAT ROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: The tour reaches the cavernous, glass-walled living room. Brent stands dead center, furiously stomping his designer shoes on the polished hardwood. He screams that the house is a hollow, worthless piece of garbage and threatens to call Richard Sterling to have Valerie blacklisted from the luxury market. Valerie's plastic smile finally falters. Her hands shake as she grips her clipboard, taking a slow, terrified step backward as Brent advances on her.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The tipping point. Brent poses a direct, existential threat to Valerie's career and survival, inadvertently triggering the house's defensive/predatory instinct.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GREAT ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Without a single warning, the hardwood floorboards beneath Brent's feet violently liquefy. A massive, fleshy maw lined with jagged, impossible teeth erupts from the wood. Brent's screams are cut short as the house swallows him whole in a single, wet gulp. The floor instantly snaps back into pristine, polished wood. The concussive shockwave of the jaw snapping shut throws Valerie off her feet, slamming her hard against the glass wall.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Catalyst. The Monster is fully revealed, shattering Valerie's corporate reality and executing a massive genre pivot into supernatural horror.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Dead silence rings through the house. Valerie scrambles on her hands and knees, hyperventilating, her designer suit scuffed. She lunges for the heavy front door, grabbing the handle and yanking with all her might. It does not budge. The smart-lock glows an angry, solid red. She slams her fists against the reinforced glass, trapped. Trembling violently, she fumbles her cell phone out of her pocket, her shaking thumb punching in 9-1-1.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Pure survival instinct. Isolates the protagonist with the monster, heightening the horror and forcing Valerie to confront the reality of her situation.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GREAT ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's thumb hovers over the call button. Suddenly, a deep, guttural shudder vibrates through the soles of her shoes, drawing her terrified gaze back to the center of the Great Room. The floorboards ripple like water. With a sickening, wet hacking sound, the house regurgitates a single object. It hits the wood with a heavy, crystalline clink and rolls across the floor, coming to a dead stop mere inches from Valerie's knees.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Complication. The house presents a literal, physical manifestation of Valerie's ultimate desire born from horrific violence, setting up the moral crisis.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stares down at a massive, flawless diamond. Her phone screen illuminates her face, the numbers 9-1-1 glaring back at her. The ticking clock of her crushing financial ruin visibly collides with the horrific reality of the murder. Her panicked, ragged breathing slowly evens out. She deliberately presses 'Cancel' and lets the phone drop to the floor. She crawls forward, picks up the heavy gemstone, and casually uses the sleeve of her expensive blazer to wipe a faint trace of ethereal saliva from its facets. She stands up tall, clutching the diamond, and surveys the hungry room with a cold, calculating smirk.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Break into Two. Valerie actively chooses greed over morality, sealing her dark pact with the entity and launching the narrative into Act II.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. VALERIE'S APARTMENT - MORNING
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie sits cross-legged on her bare mattress, furiously typing on her laptop. She hits 'AUTHORIZE.' The screen refreshes, turning a sea of red negative numbers into a flat, beautiful '$0.00.' Valerie lets out a feral, breathless laugh. She slams the laptop shut, grabs a half-empty bottle of green juice, chugs it, and tosses the plastic bottle over her shoulder as she strides toward her closet with newly minted arrogance.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Payoff. Establishes the immediate, intoxicating reward of Valerie's dark pact, erasing her primary source of vulnerability (debt) and replacing it with bulletproof confidence.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie struts through the sterile office like a conquering queen. She stops at Toby's cluttered desk and tosses a heavy velvet box onto his keyboard. Toby nervously opens it to reveal a gleaming Rolex. He stammers, instinctively pushing his glasses up his nose, and pulls out a thick, tabbed binder labeled 'Affordable Housing Initiative.' As he slides it toward her, Valerie's eyes lock onto a red-tabbed section marked 'Corrupt Zoning Officials.'
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the B-Story complication. Valerie uses transactional wealth to buy Toby's complicity, masking her predatory needs behind his altruistic goals.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - TOBY'S DESK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Toby eagerly flips open the binder, pointing to a glossy photo of Councilwoman Elena Rostova. He babbles about her history of displacing low-income tenants. Valerie leans in close, tapping a manicured nail directly on Rostova's face. A cold, predatory smile spreads across her lips. She physically yanks the entire dossier from Toby's hands, spinning on her heels and barking over her shoulder for him to draft 'exclusive, VIP-only invitations.'
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Pivot. Valerie weaponizes Toby's empathy, turning his moral crusade into a literal hit list for the monster.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Councilwoman Rostova runs her diamond-ringed fingers over the pristine marble island, sneering at the finish. Valerie stands safely ten feet away by the refrigerator, gripping her clipboard tightly. Without warning, the massive marble island splits violently down the middle. A churning vortex of jagged drywall, rebar, and fleshy teeth erupts upward. Rostova screams, her designer heels slipping uselessly on the polished floor as she is sucked into the grinding maw. The island snaps shut.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Fulfills the premise's promise. Kicks off the 'Fun and Games' montage by executing a high-status, corrupt target in a creatively horrific, architectural way.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Silence falls over the kitchen. The sink's garbage disposal gurgles wetly. With a sharp *clink*, a flawless, fist-sized diamond shoots out of the drain and bounces across the floor, stopping at Valerie's feet. Valerie casually bends down, scoops up the gem, drops it into her designer blazer pocket, and checks off a name on her clipboard.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Reinforces the transactional nature of the horror. Valerie is completely desensitized to the violence, focused entirely on the financial yield.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - INFINITY POOL - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: A toxic Crypto-Billionaire wades in the infinity pool, screaming aggressively into a waterproof phone. Valerie watches from the safety of the patio, casually sipping sparkling water. The pool's edges rapidly, silently close in. The water turns thick and viscous. The billionaire thrashes, his phone flying onto the concrete as he is pulled under. The pool completely solidifies into a flat concrete slab. A diamond shoots out of the skimmer basket directly into Valerie's waiting hand.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalates the montage. Demonstrates the house's shapeshifting abilities and Valerie's growing comfort with the slaughter.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - VAULTED HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: A predatory Hedge Fund Manager paces the narrow hallway, complaining loudly about the acoustics. Valerie steps backward out of the corridor, pulling the door shut behind her. Through the glass panels, she watches as the vaulted ceiling drops with pneumatic speed, flattening the man instantly. The ceiling retracts, leaving no blood. A diamond drops from the recessed lighting. Valerie catches it in her purse and walks away.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Climaxes the 'Fun and Games' montage. Establishes the mechanical, bloodless efficiency of the house's feeding process.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - RICHARD'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Richard Sterling pours vintage champagne, his dead eyes locked on Valerie as he hands her a crystal flute. He delivers a low, measured monologue about 'apex predators' and 'market forces.' Valerie sips her drink, looking through the glass walls of his office to her new corner suite, which is overflowing with congratulatory floral arrangements. In the background, Julian Vance aggressively snaps a pencil in half at his cubicle.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The False Victory. Valerie achieves her ultimate conscious desire (wealth and status), completely ignoring the moral rot at the core of her success.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GREAT ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, wearing expensive silk loungewear, hums to herself while using a microfiber cloth to wipe a tiny smudge off the floating glass banister. She pats the wall affectionately, treating the deadly structure like a tamed, obedient pet. She turns to pour herself another glass of champagne from a bottle resting on the floorboards.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Sets up the turn. Showcases Valerie's absolute hubris and false sense of control right before the rug is pulled out from under her.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GREAT ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: A deep, unnatural groan vibrates through the floor, knocking over Valerie's champagne bottle. The pristine white drywall suddenly splinters like dry skin. Thick, black, viscous blood oozes from the fresh cracks, dripping down and ruining a $10,000 imported Persian rug. Valerie drops her microfiber cloth, her confident smirk vanishing as the entire house violently shudders.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Reversal. The monster rejects Valerie's illusion of control, visually corrupting the pristine aesthetic to signal its escalating hunger.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie scrambles backward toward the front door. With a deafening, synchronized *SLAM*, heavy steel security shutters crash down over every floor-to-ceiling window. The front door's smart-lock flashes an angry red and deadbolts itself. Valerie lunges at the door, pounding her manicured fists against the reinforced glass until her knuckles bruise, plunging the room into claustrophobic shadows.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Raises the stakes to physical survival. Traps the protagonist, stripping away her corporate armor and reducing her to primal panic.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GREAT ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The floorboards physically undulate, tilting at a sharp angle to slide Valerie toward the center of the room. The fleshy maw rips open in the wood, emitting a rancid, hot breath. Valerie digs her fingernails into the splintering floorboards to stop her slide. Hyperventilating, she screams out a desperate bargain: 'A gala! Fifty people! The worst people you've ever tasted!' The floorboards instantly snap flat. The maw vanishes. Valerie collapses onto the wood, gasping for air, realizing she is the pet.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Midpoint/Pact escalation. Valerie crosses a massive moral threshold to save her own life, promising mass murder and cementing her status as the house's captive.
